changed_functions.name: DepFSPreCreate
key_changes: CRITICAL: Added missing SeAccessCheck authorization in the post-startup-applications-complete code path (new else branch when FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() returns TRUE).
Unpatched version returns FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK (rbx=1) immediately when startup apps are complete without performing ANY access check on the file create operation.

Patched version calls FltGetInstanceContext to obtain the instance context, checks Context+0x78 for a security descriptor, then calls IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping() and SeAccessCheck() with AccessMode=UserMode (1) to verify the caller's access rights.
If SeAccessCheck fails (access denied), the patched version sets NTSTATUS at callback_data+0x18 (IoStatus.Status) and returns FLT_PREOP_COMPLETE (rbx=4) to block the create.
If access granted, the patched version stores GrantedAccess at file_object security context offset 0x14 and clears denied access bits (GrantedAccess | 0x2000000) at offset 0x10.
Three new imports added: SeAccessCheck, FltQuerySecurityObject, IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping.
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a missing authorization/access check vulnerability (CWE-862). The FsDepends minifilter driver intercepts IRP_MJ_CREATE operations on managed volumes. After system startup applications complete, the unpatched version allows ANY caller to create/access files without verifying their access rights against the volume's security descriptor. This means a low-privileged user can open files on protected volumes that would normally be denied by the filesystem's own security checks, because the minifilter grants unconditional success. The patched version adds SeAccessCheck with the volume's security descriptor (queried during InstanceSetup via FltQuerySecurityObject and stored at instance context offset 0x78) to enforce proper authorization.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED DepFSPreCreate key path (no access check when startup apps complete):
0x1c0007034: call    qword [rel 0x1c0006238]  ; FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete()
0x1c0007040: test    al, al
0x1c0007042: jne     0x1c0007185              ; if TRUE -> return rbx=1 (SUCCESS), NO CHECK
...
0x1c0007185: mov     rbp, qword [rsp+0x38]
0x1c000718a: mov     eax, ebx                  ; eax = 1 (FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK)
0x1c000718c: mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x30]
0x1c0007191: mov     rsi, qword [rsp+0x40]
0x1c0007196: add     rsp, 0x20
0x1c000719a: pop     rdi
0x1c000719b: retn

PATCHED DepFSPreCreate new else branch (access check added):
0x1c000704b: test    al, al
0x1c0007051: je      0x1c00071c3              ; if FALSE -> old path
                                            ; if TRUE -> falls through to access check
0x1c000706d: mov     cl, byte [rsi+0x50]
0x1c0007070: test    cl, cl
0x1c000708b: call    qword [rel 0x1c00060b8]  ; FltGetInstanceContext()
...
0x1c00070bd: cmp     qword [rax+0x78], 0x0    ; check security descriptor exists
0x1c00070c2: je      0x1c0007176              ; skip if no SD
0x1c00070c8: call    qword [rel 0x1c0006268]  ; IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping()
0x1c0007111: call    qword [rel 0x1c0006278]  ; SeAccessCheck()
0x1c000711d: test    al, al
0x1c000711f: je      0x1c000713d              ; if access DENIED -> trace + error
0x1c0007121: mov     rcx, qword [rdi+0x8]     ; access GRANTED path
0x1c0007125: mov     eax, dword [rbp-0x10]    ; GrantedAccess
0x1c0007128: mov     dword [rcx+0x14], eax   ; store in security context
0x1c0007132: bts     eax, 0x19
0x1c0007136: not     eax
0x1c0007138: and     dword [rcx+0x10], eax   ; clear denied bits
...
0x1c00072fe: mov     dword [rsi+0x18], ecx   ; IoStatus.Status = AccessStatus
0x1c0007301: mov     ebx, 0x4                 ; FLT_PREOP_COMPLETE (block create)

changed_functions.name: DepFSInstanceSetup
key_changes: Added allocation and querying of the volume's security descriptor (via FltQuerySecurityObject) stored at instance context offset 0x78.
Stack frame increased from 0x90 to 0xa0 (16 additional bytes) for new local variables including BufferSizeNeeded and security descriptor buffer pointer.
New calls to FltQuerySecurityObject at 0x1c000f4a3 and 0x1c000f4fc to retrieve the volume security descriptor.
Stores the queried security descriptor at Context+0x78 (mov [rax+78h], r15 at 0x1c000f51b) for use by DepFSPreCreate's new access check.
New call to ExAllocatePoolWithTag at 0x1c000f4c4 with tag 0x73447044 to allocate the buffer for the security descriptor.
changed_functions.security_notes: This function populates the security descriptor (Context+0x78) that the patched DepFSPreCreate uses for SeAccessCheck. The unpatched version never sets up this field, meaning no security descriptor was available for access checking during pre-create.

changed_functions.name: DepFSCleanupVolumeContext
key_changes: Added cleanup code (at 0x1c001041f) to free the security descriptor at instance context offset 0x78: ExFreePoolWithTag(P, 0x73447044) at 0x1c001042d, followed by zeroing Context+0x78 (and qword [rdi+78h], 0 at 0x1c0010439).
Pool tag 0x73447044 ('DpDs' in ASCII) used for the security descriptor allocation.
This is the teardown counterpart to the InstanceSetup allocation and the DepFSPreCreate usage.
changed_functions.security_notes: Proper cleanup of the newly-added security descriptor field prevents resource leak. The patched version adds: if (*(arg1 + 0x78)) { ExFreePoolWithTag(P, 0x73447044); *(arg1 + 0x78) = 0; } before releasing the resource lock.

security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_CREATE minifilter pre-operation callback (registered via DepFSRegisterFilters -> FltRegisterFilter)
call_chain: Any file open/create (NtCreateFile/CreateFile) on a volume managed by FsDepends minifilter
Filter Manager dispatches IRP_MJ_CREATE to DepFSPreCreate
If TRUE (post-startup): unpatched returns FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK without access check
security_findings.description: The FsDepends minifilter driver's DepFSPreCreate callback intercepts all file create operations on volumes it manages. After system startup applications complete (FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() returns TRUE), the unpatched version returns FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK (value 1) unconditionally, granting the create operation to proceed WITHOUT verifying the caller's access rights against the volume's security descriptor. The patched version adds a complete SeAccessCheck path: it retrieves the instance context via FltGetInstanceContext, checks the volume security descriptor stored at Context+0x78 (populated during DepFSInstanceSetup via FltQuerySecurityObject), then calls IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping() and SeAccessCheck() with AccessMode=UserMode (1) to validate the caller's token against the security descriptor. If access is denied, it sets IoStatus.Status to the failure NTSTATUS and returns FLT_PREOP_COMPLETE (value 4) to block the operation. In the unpatched build this driver-level authorization gate is entirely absent, so the minifilter imposes no restriction of its own on creates to managed dependency volumes in steady state. Note that a minifilter returning FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK does not override the underlying filesystem's own access check; what is missing in the unpatched build is the minifilter's additional check against the managed volume's security descriptor.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. System must have completed startup applications (FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() returns TRUE) — this is the normal steady-state after boot.
2. The target volume must be managed by the FsDepends minifilter driver (the driver registers as a filesystem filter for specific volume types, typically virtual disk/VHD-backed volumes given the RtlIsCloudFilesPlaceholder import and FsRtlQueryMaximumVirtualDiskNestingLevel import).
3. The create operation must target a file on such a managed volume where the DACL would normally deny access to the calling user.
4. The callback data flags at arg1+0x10 must have the appropriate bits set: *(arg1+0x10)+0x50 bit 0x16 set (the volume is a managed/dependency volume), and the operation must not be a paging-file open (*(rcx+0x2a) & 6 == 0).
5. Any standard NtCreateFile/CreateFile call targeting a file on the managed volume from a low-privileged user context triggers the vulnerable path.
security_findings.primitive: Missing driver-level authorization gate on IRP_MJ_CREATE for volumes managed by the FsDepends minifilter. In steady state the unpatched minifilter does not perform its own SeAccessCheck against the managed volume's security descriptor before letting the create proceed. What is provable from the binaries is only the presence/absence of this check; no memory-corruption, information-leak, or code-execution primitive is present, and the ultimate impact depends on the contents and security descriptors of the managed dependency volumes, which are not determinable from the driver binaries.

breakpoints.why: Break on every file create operation intercepted by the FsDepends minifilter. RCX = arg1 (FLT_CALLBACK_DATA), RDX = arg2 (PFLT_RELATED_OBJECTS). Check what happens after FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete returns.

The critical conditional jump 'jne 0x1c0007185'. When AL=1 (startup apps complete), this jump is taken and the function returns FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK (1) WITHOUT any access check. In the patched version, this same condition falls through to the SeAccessCheck path instead.
The return epilogue. At this point EAX (from EBX) should be 1 (FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK). Confirm that the create is being allowed without SeAccessCheck being called.

watch: AL register at 0x1c0007040: result of FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete(). When AL=1, the vulnerable path is taken.
EBX register: holds the return value. Starts at 1 (FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK). Remains 1 when no access check is performed.
RCX at function entry (0x1c0007010): points to FLT_CALLBACK_DATA structure. RCX+0x10 = FLT_PARAMETERS for IRP_MJ_CREATE. *(RCX+0x10)+0x8 = volume object, *(RCX+0x10)+0x18 = security context.
RDX at function entry: points to PFLT_RELATED_OBJECTS. *(RDX+0x10) = file object, *(RDX+0x18) = instance handle.
key_offsets: 0x1c0007034: call FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() — the gate that determines if the access check path is taken
0x1c0007042: jne 0x1c0007185 — THE MISSING CHECK: when startup apps are complete, this jumps directly to return without any authorization
0x1c0007185: return epilogue — returns EBX=1 (FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK), allowing the create unconditionally
In PATCHED version at 0x1c00070bd: cmp qword [rax+0x78], 0x0 — checks security descriptor existence (added check)
In PATCHED version at 0x1c0007111: call SeAccessCheck — the authorization gate that was entirely missing in unpatched
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
1. Ensure the system has finished booting and startup applications are complete (normal steady state).
2. Identify a volume managed by the FsDepends minifilter (typically VHD/virtual-disk-backed volumes — the driver imports FsRtlQueryMaximumVirtualDiskNestingLevel and RtlIsCloudFilesPlaceholder, suggesting it manages dependency relationships for nested virtual disks or cloud files).
3. From a low-privileged user account, attempt to open/create a file on the managed volume that should be restricted by the DACL.
4. Use CreateFile/NtCreateFile with the target path on the managed volume.
5. In the unpatched version, the operation succeeds because DepFSPreCreate returns FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK without checking access.
6. In the patched version, the same operation fails with STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED because SeAccessCheck rejects it.
7. Attach WinDbg/kd and set 'bp fsdepends_unpatched!DepFSPreCreate' to confirm the callback fires and observe the unconditional return value of 1.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched binary: at breakpoint 0x1c0007040, AL will be 1 (startup complete). The JNE at 0x1c0007042 will be taken, jumping to 0x1c0007185. EAX will be loaded with EBX=1 (FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK), so the minifilter does not impose its own restriction on the create. No call to SeAccessCheck will occur (the function is not even imported by the unpatched binary). In the patched binary: the same path would fall through to FltGetInstanceContext -> IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping -> SeAccessCheck, and if the caller lacks rights, AccessStatus would be set to STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED and the function would return 4 (FLT_PREOP_COMPLETE).
debugging_aids.notes: The FsDepends driver (FsDepends.sys) is a Windows filesystem minifilter that manages dependency relationships between volumes, particularly for nested virtual disks and cloud files placeholder volumes. The pool tag for security descriptor allocations is 0x73447044 ('DpDs'). The instance context structure has the security descriptor at offset 0x78. The pre-startup path (when FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() returns FALSE) handles autochk/volume verification and was present in both versions — only the post-startup path lacked the access check. The function is registered as a pre-operation callback for IRP_MJ_CREATE via DepFSRegisterFilters -> FltRegisterFilter. No ASLR bypass is needed for the breakpoint since WinDbg resolves the symbol directly.

summary: The patch fixes a missing authorization/access check vulnerability (CWE-862) in FsDepends.sys, a Windows filesystem minifilter driver. The unpatched DepFSPreCreate minifilter pre-create callback unconditionally allows all file create operations on managed volumes after system startup completes (when FsRtlAreVolumeStartupApplicationsComplete() returns TRUE), returning FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK without verifying the caller's access rights. The patched version adds a complete authorization path: it retrieves the volume's security descriptor (stored at instance context+0x78, populated via FltQuerySecurityObject during DepFSInstanceSetup), calls IoGetFileObjectGenericMapping() and SeAccessCheck() with AccessMode=UserMode, and blocks the operation with FLT_PREOP_COMPLETE if access is denied. Supporting changes in DepFSInstanceSetup (allocate/query SD) and DepFSCleanupVolumeContext (free SD) manage the lifecycle of the security descriptor. A PoC would open or create a file on a FsDepends-managed volume from a low-privileged user context — the unpatched minifilter imposes no authorization check of its own, while the patched version enforces the managed volume's security descriptor. Severity is assessed as medium: the added SeAccessCheck is a genuine, reachable authorization gate, but the binaries do not demonstrate a concrete cross-privilege file-access primitive, and a minifilter returning FLT_PREOP_SUCCESS_WITH_CALLBACK does not disable the underlying filesystem's own DACL enforcement.
